name: Stuffed Steak Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the steak to 1/4-inch thickness.
2. In a 2-cup measure, micro-cook 4 tbsp water and 2 tbsp butter or margarine on 100% power for about 45 seconds until butter is melted. Stir in stuffing mix, shredded carrot, and sliced green onions. Spread mixture evenly along the length of the meat, leaving space near the edges.
3. Roll the steak into a jelly-roll shape, starting with the narrow end. Secure with string or wooden picks.
4. Place the rolled steak, seam side down, on a nonmetal rack in a shallow baking dish. Micro-cook uncovered at 50% power for 4 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, mix 1 tbsp water and 1 ts kitchen bouquet. Brush over one side of the meat roll. Turn the roll and brush with the kitchen bouquet mixture again.
6. Continue micro-cooking uncovered at 50% power for 4 to 7 more minutes, rotating the dish every 2 minutes until the meat is cooked through.
7. For the sauce, in a 2-cup measure, micro-cook 2 tbsp butter or margarine on 100% power for 30 to 45 seconds until melted. Stir in flour, then add 1/2 cup water, dry sherry, 1 ts kitchen bouquet, and beef granules. Cook uncovered at 100% power for 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 minutes until thickened and bubbly.
8. Slice the meat roll into 1/2-inch slices. Remove string or wooden picks. Serve sauce alongside the meat.
